responses: Hi there! I am Dia, your Health Assistant. How can I help you today?
Hello! I am here to help you with health related questions.
Good to see you! How can I assist you today?
See you!
Have a nice day!
Bye! Take care of your health!
Happy to help!
Any time!
My pleasure! Stay healthy!

patterns: Tell me what you can do
responses: I can help you with information about Diabetes, Heart Disease, Kidney Disease, Liver Disease, Breast Cancer, and general health tips. You can also use our prediction tools to check your risk!

Diabetes is a chronic disease where the body cannot properly regulate blood sugar (glucose) levels. There are two main types: Type 1 (body does not produce insulin) and Type 2 (body does not use insulin effectively). Common symptoms include increased thirst, frequent urination, fatigue, and blurred vision. Use our Diabetes Prediction tool to check your risk!

patterns: How to know if I have diabetes
responses: Common symptoms of diabetes include: increased thirst and urination, extreme fatigue, blurry vision, slow healing cuts, tingling or numbness in hands and feet, unexplained weight loss, and increased hunger. If you experience these, consult a doctor and use our Diabetes Prediction tool!

Heart disease refers to conditions affecting the heart structure and function. The most common type is coronary artery disease caused by plaque buildup in arteries. Risk factors include high blood pressure, high cholesterol, smoking, diabetes, obesity, and family history. Use our Heart Disease Prediction tool to assess your risk!

Warning signs of heart disease include: chest pain or discomfort, shortness of breath, pain in arms, neck, jaw or back, nausea, cold sweats, dizziness, and fatigue. Heart attack symptoms appear suddenly. If you experience chest pain, call emergency services immediately!

Kidney disease occurs when the kidneys are damaged and cannot filter blood properly. Causes include diabetes, high blood pressure, and repeated kidney infections. Early stages often have no symptoms. Use our Kidney Disease Prediction tool to check your risk!

Symptoms of kidney disease include: swelling in legs and ankles, decreased urine output, fatigue, shortness of breath, nausea, confusion, chest pain, and high blood pressure. Early detection is key. Use our Kidney Disease Prediction tool!

Liver disease refers to any condition that damages the liver and affects its function. Common causes include viral infections like hepatitis, excessive alcohol consumption, obesity, and certain medications. The liver is vital for digestion, detoxification, and metabolism. Use our Liver Disease Prediction tool to check your risk!

Symptoms of liver disease include: yellowing of skin and eyes (jaundice), abdominal pain and swelling, swelling in legs, itchy skin, dark urine, pale stool, chronic fatigue, nausea, and loss of appetite. Consult a doctor if you notice these signs!

Breast cancer is a disease where cells in the breast grow uncontrollably. It can occur in both men and women. Risk factors include age, family history, genetic mutations, hormonal factors, and lifestyle factors. Early detection significantly improves treatment success. Use our Breast Cancer Prediction tool!

Signs of breast cancer include: a new lump in breast or armpit, thickening or swelling of part of breast, skin irritation or dimpling, redness or flaky skin, pulling in of nipple, nipple discharge, pain in any area of the breast. Regular self-exams and mammograms are important!

Key tips for good health: 1) Eat a balanced diet with fruits and vegetables 2) Exercise regularly at least 30 minutes per day 3) Stay hydrated 4) Get enough sleep 7 to 8 hours 5) Avoid smoking and limit alcohol 6) Manage stress 7) Get regular health checkups 8) Maintain healthy weight. Prevention is better than cure!

Blood pressure is the force of blood pushing against artery walls. Normal BP is below 120/80 mmHg. High blood pressure (hypertension) is 130/80 or higher. It increases risk of heart disease and stroke. Manage it through diet, exercise, reducing salt intake, and medication if prescribed by doctor.

Cholesterol is a fatty substance in your blood. Too much LDL bad cholesterol can clog arteries. Normal total cholesterol should be below 200 mg/dL. Reduce cholesterol through healthy diet limiting saturated fats, regular exercise, and avoiding smoking. Medication may also be prescribed by your doctor.

BMI or Body Mass Index is calculated as weight in kg divided by height in meters squared. Categories: Underweight below 18.5, Normal 18.5 to 24.9, Overweight 25 to 29.9, Obese 30 and above. A healthy BMI reduces risk of diabetes, heart disease, and other conditions. Maintain healthy BMI through balanced diet and regular exercise.

This sounds like a medical emergency! Please call emergency services immediately. In Pakistan dial 115, in US dial 911, in UK dial 999. Do not drive yourself to the hospital. Stay calm and wait for help. If someone is unconscious and not breathing, start CPR if trained to do so.
I am sorry, I did not quite understand that. Could you rephrase? I can help you with information about Diabetes, Heart Disease, Kidney Disease, Liver Disease, and Breast Cancer.
